data.title: Market makers halted trading during Bitcoin crash, leading to $19B liquidation and increased volatility.
data.description: During the recent market turbulence, trading was temporarily halted by market makers, potentially exacerbating the volatility. This pause in trading occurred last week during a flash crash, causing concern among investors. The brief stoppage in trading contributed to a significant increase in market fluctuations, with some sectors experiencing sudden drops in prices. This interruption lasted for approximately X minutes, leading to a sharp decline in the market indices, with the X dropping by Y points within a short period of time. The incident raised questions about the role of market makers in times of heightened market uncertainty and their impact on overall market stability.

data.title: Stocks bounce back from bank fears as AXP soars. S&P 500 rises to 6,650 points. Nasdaq and Dow Jones up by 0.3%. Financials rebound. Trump optimistic on US-China trade.
data.description: Wall Street showed resilience against credit concerns impacting bank stocks, as major indices experienced slight gains during midday trading in New York, pointing towards a positive weekly conclusion. AXP emerged as one of the top performers, with the S&P 500 climbing 0.2% to reach 6,650 points, almost offsetting losses from the previous day, while both the Nasdaq 100 and the Dow Jones saw a 0.3% uptick. Following a notable downturn on Wednesday, financials and bank stocks rebounded, with the SPDR Regional Banking ETF (NYSE: KRE) rising by 1.1% and the Financials Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSE: XLF) showing a 0.5% increase. President Donald Trump expressed confidence in the economic relationship with China, suggesting that the proposed 100% tariff hike on Chinese goods might not be implemented. For further details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.
